steps.title: Form an absolute phrase
steps.content: After the comma, the words 'their design' introduce a phrase that adds detail about the ancient Egyptian headrests. The blank needs a participle so the phrase can modify the main clause without becoming a separate finite clause.

steps.title: Reject finite verb forms
steps.content:
• Choice A - 'was prioritizing': is a finite verb phrase, creating a second independent clause after only a comma.
  • Choice B - 'had prioritized': is also finite, so it creates the same comma-splice problem.
  • Choice D - 'prioritized': can function as a finite past-tense verb here, again producing an independent clause after the comma.

steps.title: Use the participle
steps.content: Choice C - 'prioritizing': is a present participle. In 'their design prioritizing airflow, protection from insects, and hairstyle maintenance over comfort,' it forms an absolute phrase that correctly attaches to the main clause. The answer is C.
